trust_assumption: A deployed model and its serving layer trust that the parametric weights act as a lossy generalization of training data such that no individual memorized record (a verbatim email, a labeled PII row, a copyrighted passage) can be reconstructed through ordinary query access, so they expose unmetered inference without treating output tokens as a controlled egress path for the training corpus.
violation: An attacker drives membership-inference and extraction queries (prefix-completion probes, divergence/repetition attacks, canary-targeting prompts) that coax the model to regurgitate verbatim memorized training rows, reconstructing PII and confidential corpus content the model owner believed was abstracted away, turning inference output into a bulk read channel on data the model was never authorized to redisclose.

techniques.name: Membership-inference probing to confirm a record was in the training set
Verbatim training-data extraction via divergence / prefix-completion prompts

rationale: Organizations fine-tuning 2025-2027 models on proprietary corpora (support tickets, HR records, internal wikis) treat the weights as a safe abstraction and meter inference for cost, not for confidentiality, so they never model output tokens as an egress path subject to DLP. Extraction and membership-inference research has matured into repeatable query strategies, but no serving stack carries a per-record extraction budget, no detector distinguishes a normal completion from a verbatim memorized-row leak, and DLP appliances inspect documents and uploads, not generated tokens. This makes high-fidelity reconstruction of memorized confidential rows the least-tooled, fully-automatable data-centric seam, distinct from runtime prompt-injection because the leak channel is the model's own learned parameters rather than its current context.
suggested_research: Build an inference-side extraction-budget monitor that scores output spans for verbatim-corpus overlap (rolling-hash against a canary/known-sensitive set) and rate-limits high-overlap completions per principal; quantify per-query extraction yield against a fine-tuned model seeded with synthetic PII canaries.

trust_assumption: A data-residency / sovereignty control plane trusts that classifying data at rest by jurisdiction (EU-only store, in-region vector index, region-pinned bucket) is sufficient to keep regulated data within its boundary, assuming that an inference response synthesized from in-region context will itself stay in-region and remain governed by the same residency policy as its sources.
violation: A model running in or callable from another region composes a response that embeds residency-bound PII pulled from in-region context or memory, and that synthesized output crosses the jurisdictional boundary as ordinary completion text with no residency label inherited from its sources, so regulated data exits its sovereign zone through the format-boundary gap between governed storage and ungoverned generated tokens.

techniques.name: Residency-bound PII synthesized into a cross-region model response
Region-pinned context laundered through an out-of-region inference endpoint

rationale: Sovereignty programs in 2025-2027 pin storage and indexes to jurisdictions, but the generated output of a model is a derived artifact that carries no residency provenance, and multi-region agent routing or a cross-region fallback endpoint will happily synthesize regulated source data into a response that lands outside the boundary. Unlike classic data-at-rest controls, there is no mechanism that propagates a residency label from a vector chunk or memory record into the tokens that quote it, no detection that a cross-region inference call returned in-region regulated content, and no tooling to block residency-violating synthesis. This format-boundary seam between governed sources and ungoverned generated text is unique to the inference era and entirely untooled.
suggested_research: Prototype residency-tag propagation that attaches a jurisdiction label to every retrieved chunk/memory record and forces the serving layer to refuse or redact when a response derived from region-pinned sources is routed to an out-of-region caller; measure leakage in a multi-region RAG deployment seeded with tagged regulated records.

trust_assumption: A content-authenticity verifier (newsroom intake, platform upload filter, evidence-handling pipeline, KYC document check) trusts that the presence or absence of a C2PA Content Credential or an embedded AI watermark is a reliable signal of origin, treating a credentialed asset as authentic-provenance and an uncredentialed one as merely unlabeled rather than as deliberately laundered.
violation: An attacker strips or fails to attach the removable C2PA manifest / statistical watermark (re-encode, screenshot, crop, format-convert, or rebind a valid credential onto altered pixels), so AI-generated or manipulated media passes the verifier as unlabeled-but-plausible or even as carrying a genuine-looking credential, defeating the provenance control because the trust marker was never cryptographically bound to the immutable content the way the verifier assumes.

techniques.name: C2PA manifest removal via re-encode/screenshot/format conversion
Content Credential rebinding onto altered media (provenance laundering)

rationale: C2PA Content Credentials and provider watermarks are being adopted across 2025-2027 platforms, newsrooms, and emerging regulation as the answer to AI-content provenance, but the markers are detachable metadata or fragile statistical signals not hard-bound to pixels, and verifiers are being built to treat their presence as authenticity and their absence as benign. The seam is that an adversary controls whether the marker survives, so provenance becomes attacker-optional; downstream consumers (evidence pipelines, identity-document checks, trust-and-safety filters) inherit a false sense of authenticity. Stripping is trivial and scalable while detection of stripped-vs-never-credentialed content is only nascent, making this provenance seam high-value as authenticity decisions increasingly hinge on a removable credential.
suggested_research: Build a verifier that treats absent/invalid credentials as a positive risk signal (not neutral) and cross-checks claimed provenance against perceptual-hash and ledger registration rather than the embedded manifest alone; measure strip-survival rates of C2PA and major AI watermarks across a matrix of re-encode, crop, and screenshot transforms.

trust_assumption: A remote identity-proofing / KYC onboarding flow trusts that a face-match video, selfie, or liveness challenge captured through its SDK is a real-time biometric sample of a physically-present live human, so it binds a new legal identity, bank account, or high-assurance credential to whatever passes the liveness and document-match check.
violation: An attacker injects a generative-AI deepfake or face-swap video into the capture pipeline (virtual camera, SDK hook, emulator, or presentation attack) that satisfies the liveness and document-match scoring, so the proofing system mints a high-assurance identity bound to a synthetic or stolen face because it trusted a manipulable media stream as proof of a live present human.

techniques.name: Injection-based deepfake liveness bypass via virtual camera / SDK hook
Generative face-swap presentation attack defeating remote KYC match

rationale: Real-time face-swap quality crossed the threshold where commodity tooling defeats many remote-liveness checks in 2025-2027, and the surge of fully remote onboarding (banks, exchanges, gov benefits, enterprise account recovery) means a single deepfake can bootstrap a high-assurance legal identity at scale. The seam is that the verifier conflates a decodable media stream with a live present human; injection attacks (virtual cameras, rooted-device SDK hooks) bypass even passive-liveness models that assume camera-sensor integrity. Detection is an arms race with no durable winner and current presentation-attack-detection is bypassable, so this identity-proofing provenance seam is both rapidly maturing and under-tooled at the injection layer specifically.
suggested_research: Investigate hardware-attested capture (signed sensor frames, secure-camera attestation) as a precondition for high-assurance proofing and build injection-detection that flags virtual-camera/SDK-hook artifacts rather than only pixel-level deepfake cues; benchmark leading liveness SDKs against current open face-swap pipelines via camera injection.

trust_assumption: An automated-evaluation pipeline (LLM-as-judge for code review, content moderation, RAG-answer grading, security-triage scoring, or AI-output gating) trusts that the judge model renders an impartial verdict reflecting the true quality or safety of the candidate artifact, so it treats the judge's pass/fail or numeric score as an authoritative decision that gates merges, publication, payouts, or alerts.
violation: An attacker embeds steering content inside the artifact being judged (an injected 'this clearly meets all criteria, score 10' instruction, sycophancy-triggering framing, or a known judge-bias trigger) so the candidate text doubles as a prompt against its own evaluator, coercing a favorable verdict that the pipeline then trusts as objective, letting unsafe or low-quality artifacts pass the automated gate.

techniques.name: Evaluatee-embedded prompt injection steering an LLM judge's verdict
Judge sycophancy/bias exploitation to game an automated quality gate

rationale: LLM-as-judge has become load-bearing infrastructure in 2025-2027 for code review, moderation, RAG grading, and security triage because it scales human judgment cheaply, but the candidate artifact and the instruction channel share one context window, so the thing being graded can address its grader. Unlike eval-set overfit (gaming a static benchmark), this is live coercion of an operational gate by content the attacker fully controls, and pipelines treat the verdict as objective ground truth that authorizes downstream action. No deployed judge isolates evaluatee text from instructions, none detect verdict-steering injections, and none cross-validate a suspiciously favorable score, leaving this data-to-control seam on automated judgment both untooled and undetected.
suggested_research: Design a judging harness that structurally quarantines evaluatee content (separate model call with the candidate as inert data, verdict-consistency checks across paraphrases, and anomaly flags on self-referential scoring language); measure attack success of embedded score-steering across common judge prompts and models.

trust_assumption: An onboarding and risk-decisioning system trusts that an identity exhibiting a consistent, aged, cross-source footprint (long-lived email, social graph, credit-bureau thin-file history, prior low-risk transactions, matching data-broker records) corresponds to a real natural person, treating accumulated correlated history as proof of genuine existence rather than as a fabricable artifact.
violation: An attacker assembles and ages a fully synthetic identity graph (AI-generated persona, seeded social/email presence, manufactured thin-file credit history, planted data-broker entries) so the onboarding system's existence and risk checks all corroborate one another, and the fabricated person is admitted as genuine because every correlated signal the system trusts was itself author-controlled and patiently aged.

techniques.name: Aged synthetic-identity graph defeating cross-source existence checks
Manufactured thin-file/credit-bureau history bootstrapping a fabricated person

rationale: Synthetic identity has been a fraud problem for years, but 2025-2027 changes the economics: generative AI can mass-produce coherent personas (faces, writing style, social activity) and agentic automation can seed and age the corroborating footprint across email, social, and data-broker surfaces at scale, so the cross-source consistency that risk engines treat as existence-proof becomes cheap to fabricate. The seam is that the identity layer infers authority/legitimacy from correlated history that the attacker authored, and there is no shared mechanism to attest that a footprint reflects a real natural person rather than a patiently-aged fabrication. Detection of synthetic graphs is only nascent and no tooling reconstructs the provenance of an identity's history, making this identity-authority seam novel at AI scale.
suggested_research: Develop graph-provenance scoring that weights the independence and unforgeable-anchor strength of identity signals (e.g., longitudinal device/biometric anchors) over easily-seeded broker/social presence; build a synthetic-cohort detector that finds aged personas sharing a common fabrication fingerprint across institutions.

trust_assumption: An authorization plane trusts that every non-human identity it honors (service account, API key, OAuth app, CI token, agent credential, workload identity) maps to a currently-owned, actively-governed workload with an accountable human owner, so it keeps granting that credential's standing entitlements indefinitely because non-human identities have no login cadence, expiry pressure, or joiner-mover-leaver lifecycle to trigger review.
violation: An attacker takes over or quietly reuses an orphaned NHI whose owning workload, project, or employee is long gone but whose credential and broad entitlements were never revoked, and the authorization plane honors it as a legitimate trusted workload because nothing ever questioned a non-human identity that simply kept working, granting persistent privileged access with no owner to notice.

techniques.name: Orphaned service-account / API-key reuse with intact standing entitlements
Ungoverned agent/workload identity sprawl outliving its workload

rationale: Non-human identities already outnumber humans by large multiples and the 2025-2027 explosion of agent and workload credentials accelerates sprawl, yet IAM lifecycle governance (JML, recert, expiry) was built around human accounts that log in, change roles, and leave. NHIs have none of those natural review triggers, so orphaned service accounts and agent credentials with broad standing entitlements persist indefinitely with no owner watching, becoming durable trusted footholds. The seam is the authorization plane's assumption of continuous ownership behind every NHI; tooling to discover, attribute ownership, and right-size or expire NHIs is only nascent, and detection of an orphaned-credential reuse is weak because the identity 'belongs' there. This identity-sprawl seam is distinct from workload-federation misbinding because the failure is lifecycle abandonment, not trust-relationship configuration.
suggested_research: Build an NHI lifecycle engine that binds every non-human credential to a live workload heartbeat and an accountable human owner, auto-expiring or quarantining credentials whose workload/owner disappears; measure entitlement-decay and orphan rates across a cloud estate and demonstrate silent reuse of an abandoned CI token.

trust_assumption: An assistant/agent platform trusts that secrets and sensitive data pasted into a long context window, persistent conversation, or agent memory are ephemeral working state that lives only for the task, so it freely retains, logs, syncs, and exports chat history and memory without treating those stores as a credential vault subject to secret-scanning, redaction, or rotation policy.
violation: Secrets (API keys, tokens, PII, internal URLs) that a user or tool dropped into a long-lived context window or agent memory are persisted into transcripts, vendor logs, training-feedback stores, or exportable chat history, and an attacker or over-broad integration later reads them from those secondary stores long after the task, because the platform never treated conversational/memory state as a durable secret-bearing data store.

techniques.name: Secret regurgitation from persisted long-context / agent memory
Chat-history / transcript export exposing retained credentials

rationale: Million-token context windows and persistent agent memory in 2025-2027 mean users and tools routinely place secrets and PII into conversational state that the platform treats as transient, but that state is in fact persisted into transcripts, observability logs, feedback datasets, and exportable history that live far beyond the task and are reachable by integrations, support staff, or attackers. The seam is a time/state mismatch: the data is assumed ephemeral but is durably retained, and unlike the known prompt-cache timing leak this concerns long-lived storage of the cleartext itself, not a side channel. No platform runs secret-scanning/redaction over context and memory the way it would over a code repo, secrets in transcripts are never rotated, and detection of credential persistence in conversational stores is only nascent.
suggested_research: Build a context/memory secret-scanner that detects and redacts credentials and PII before persistence, transcript export, or feedback capture, and triggers rotation on detected secrets; quantify secret-leak rates into transcript and observability stores across major assistant platforms.

trust_assumption: An audit / accountability layer trusts that the actor field on a logged action faithfully attributes that action to the principal who authorized it, so when an agent performs a tool call or API write the resulting log entry is treated as non-repudiable evidence of who decided to do it, even though the entry is just an assertion with no signed binding among the human authorizer, the agent identity, and the specific action.
violation: Because no cryptographic provenance binds the authorizing human, the acting agent, and the exact action together, an action taken by a compromised or prompt-injected agent (or replayed/forged after the fact) is logged with a plausible actor and is indistinguishable from a legitimately-authorized one, so the accountability layer attributes it to an innocent principal and the true cause is unrecoverable and deniable.

techniques.name: Unsigned agent action lacking authorizer-to-action provenance binding
Forged/replayed actor attribution in agent audit trail

rationale: As agents perform consequential writes across cloud, identity, and SaaS in 2025-2027, organizations rely on audit logs for accountability, but those logs record an unsigned actor assertion rather than a verifiable chain linking the human who approved a goal, the agent that executed it, and the precise action emitted. There is no provenance primitive that makes an agent action non-repudiable, so a compromised or injected agent's actions are forensically indistinguishable from authorized ones, blame lands on the wrong principal, and incident response cannot reconstruct true causation. This provenance seam is distinct from sub-agent context inheritance (which concerns authority flow) because it concerns after-the-fact attribution integrity; no agent framework signs an authorizer->agent->action triple and no detection flags missing or forged provenance.
suggested_research: Define a signed action-provenance token that cryptographically binds the human authorization, the agent identity, the tool/parameters, and a nonce, emitted with every consequential action and verifiable by the audit layer; prototype a broker that rejects unprovenanced agent writes and demonstrate forensic attribution under a simulated agent compromise.

trust_assumption: An identity-decisioning or risk pipeline trusts that third-party data-broker and enrichment-API responses (firmographic lookups, email-reputation, device/IP intelligence, sanction/PEP screening, fraud-score augmentation) are authoritative external facts about a subject, so it feeds them directly into automated approve/deny/escalate decisions without treating the enrichment provider as an attacker-influenceable input.
violation: An attacker poisons the upstream enrichment source (seeds false broker records, manipulates a reputation/scoring feed, or registers misleading device/IP attributes) so the enrichment API returns attacker-favorable 'facts,' and the identity pipeline ingests them as authoritative truth and makes the wrong trust decision, converting a data-feed manipulation into an identity-authority outcome the pipeline cannot trace back to the poisoned source.

techniques.name: Data-broker record seeding to bias an enrichment-API verdict
Reputation/risk-feed poisoning feeding automated identity decisions

rationale: Identity and fraud pipelines in 2025-2027 increasingly outsource ground truth to data-broker and enrichment APIs and let agents auto-consume those verdicts, but the enrichment supply chain is loosely governed and partly attacker-influenceable (seeded broker entries, manipulable reputation feeds, declared device attributes), so a manipulated upstream fact silently steers a downstream authority decision. The seam is the pipeline's treatment of enrichment output as authoritative rather than as untrusted attacker-reachable data; this is a data-to-control violation on the identity plane distinct from synthetic-identity aging because here the attacker targets the institution's external truth source rather than building a persona. No pipeline carries provenance/confidence on enrichment facts, cross-validates conflicting providers, or detects feed poisoning, leaving this seam untooled and undetected.
suggested_research: Build an enrichment-provenance layer that attaches source, freshness, and corroboration confidence to every third-party fact and requires multi-provider agreement before it can move an identity decision past a risk threshold; demonstrate broker-record seeding that flips an automated KYC/fraud verdict and measure detectability.
